page: The UpCloud 6xCPU-16GB is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features 6 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, with 6 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.67 GB of memory per core, and includes 320 GB of local SSD storage. It also comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile for this configuration. The shared CPU allocation results in poor single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and weak multi-core CPU performance. However, Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also perform at an average level, though the Passmark memory score is weak.

This server is suited for workloads that require moderate memory and multi-core capabilities but do not demand sustained, high-performance CPU execution. Suitable applications include basic database operations, Redis caching, and text processing, which show average benchmark results. It is less optimal for high-throughput static web serving or medium-to-large language model inference due to weak performance in those areas. The shared CPU architecture offers a cost-efficient resource density for development environments and non-critical utility services where CPU contention is acceptable.
description: The UpCloud 6xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 6 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local SSD storage. While its shared CPU allocation leads to poor single-core and weak multi-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks, it delivers average results in Geekbench, Passmark CPU, and Redis SET operations. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective option for workloads requiring moderate memory and multi-core capacity without continuous high CPU utilization. It is best suited for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose utility tasks, though it is less effective for high-throughput web serving or intensive LLM inference.
og_description: UpCloud 6xCPU-16GB is a general-purpose KVM server with 6 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB SSD storage, offering balanced average performance for databases and utility workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud 6xCPU-16GB is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 6 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB SSD storage for utility workloads.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server offering balanced memory and local SSD storage for moderate database operations and utility workloads.
bullet_points: 6 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 320 GB of local SSD storage
KVM hypervisor-based virtualization on x86_64 architecture
Average performance in Geekbench and Passmark CPU benchmarks
Best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and software compilation
Shared CPU allocation provides a cost-effective profile for non-critical workloads
